title: Cross-Merchant Synthetic Identity Layering with Shared Fragment Rotation
narrative: Fraudster constructs a synthetic identity by systematically rotating fragments (email, billing address, card BIN) across 8-12 merchants over 45-90 days to build apparent legitimacy. Pattern begins with low-risk MCCs (grocery 5411, gas 5542) using card1 paired with P_emaildomain yahoo.com and addr1 block 247. After 15-20 transactions totaling $340-$890, actor pivots to same card1 but switches to P_emaildomain outlook.com with addr1 block 522 at electronics MCC 5732, establishing a second fragment combination. Final phase reunites original email with new address at high-ticket MCC 5944 (jewelry) for $1,800-$3,200 purchases. The card1 appears legitimate via distributed history, but no single merchant sees the full fragment matrix. Each merchant validates only their slice, missing the synthetic assembly.
indicators: card1 used across 8+ distinct MCCs within 60 days with card1_mcc_diversity_score >0.72
P_emaildomain switches 2-3 times for same card1, each domain sustained 12-18 days before rotation
addr1 changes but maintains same dist1 range (420-580 km) suggesting controlled geographic fabrication
Initial merchant phase shows 15-20 txns with TransactionAmt $12-$67, then sudden jump to $1,800+ within 5 days
card1_addr1_combination_count equals 3-4 unique pairs, but card1_email_combination_count also 3-4, creating 9-16 permutations
No single MID sees more than 2 fragment combinations, card1_per_merchant_fragment_diversity <0.35
Final high-value transactions occur 48-72 hours after last fragment rotation, txn_since_fragment_change <4320 minutes
summary: A card appears across 8+ merchant categories over 60-90 days with systematically rotating email domains and billing addresses, where no single merchant sees more than 2 combinations but the card accumulates 9-16 total fragment permutations. Transaction amounts escalate from $12-67 across groceries and gas to sudden $1,800+ jewelry purchases within days of the final address/email swap.
reasoning: The fraudster is building a fake credit history by spreading fragment combinations thin so each merchant's fraud checks see only a narrow, normal-looking slice, masking that the underlying identity is fabricated from mismatched stolen or invented data. The controlled distance ranges and timed rotations reveal deliberate orchestration rather than a real person naturally changing details, with the high-value final purchases exploiting the synthetic legitimacy once enough distributed history exists.
indicator_explanations: Card appears across numerous unrelated merchant categories indicating profile layering activity
Email address rotates through distinct domains with deliberate timing gaps between switches
Address changes but distances stay suspiciously consistent suggesting fabricated geographic spread
Spending pattern shifts from small trust-building purchases to sudden large amounts
Identity fragments create multiple fake personas through systematic address and email mixing
Each merchant sees limited fragment combinations preventing pattern detection across sellers
Large purchases occur shortly after identity details stabilize following rotation cycle
